author: dashboard-bootstrap
content: CI failure follow-up: the 3 new integration tests assumed 'timeout' is on PATH. The CI runner that failed had neither 'timeout' nor 'gtimeout' (likely macOS without 'brew install coreutils', or a stripped image). Added resolve_test_timeout_cmd() helper that probes for either binary via --version and returns Some(name) when usable, None otherwise. Each test now early-returns with an eprintln skip message when no usable timeout binary is available — passes cleanly instead of false-failing. Also replaced the sandbox passthrough stub from 'env --' (GNU-specific, BSD env may reject) to a tempdir-written shell script ('#!/bin/sh\nexec "$\@"') so the sandbox test is portable across macOS too. cargo fmt clean, clippy clean (resolved a manual-find-implementation warning along the way), all 11 build_agent_command tests pass locally with timeout present.
